channel.title: BuildStack
channel.description: BuildStack is an independent publication covering web development, AI tools, and developer guides. Honest, practical writing from working engineers — no fluff, no clickbait.

item.title: How to Build Authentication in Next.js: A Step-by-Step Walkthrough
item.description: A practical, opinionated guide to adding email + OAuth authentication to a Next.js App Router project — covering sessions, middleware, and the real-world gotchas.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: The Best AI Tools for Developers in 2026 (Used Daily, Not Just Tested)
item.description: An honest look at the AI tools that have actually changed how we work in 2026 — what they're great at, where they fall apart, and how to use them without becoming dependent on them.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: How to Optimize Core Web Vitals Without Breaking a Sweat
item.description: A practical playbook for shipping fast websites in 2026 — what each Core Web Vital actually measures, the fixes that move the needle, and the ones that quietly don't.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: A Practical Beginner's Guide to REST APIs
item.description: What a REST API actually is, why HTTP verbs matter, and how to design and consume one without falling into common traps. Written for developers who want a working mental model, not a textbook definition.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: How to Deploy a Full-Stack App: A No-Nonsense Walkthrough
item.description: From localhost to a live URL — a clear, modern path to deploying a Next.js + Postgres app without buying into a single vendor's worldview.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Mastering React Server Components in Next.js
item.description: Server Components have changed how Next.js apps are built. Here's a working mental model — what they're for, where Client Components belong, and the patterns that scale.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: TypeScript Patterns That Make Your Codebase Easier to Live With
item.description: Beyond the basics — a handful of TypeScript patterns that pay rent every day, including discriminated unions, branded types, and how to stop fighting the compiler.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Tailwind CSS vs CSS-in-JS: How to Actually Choose
item.description: A grown-up comparison of Tailwind, CSS Modules, and CSS-in-JS — what each is good at, where each falls down, and which one fits your team.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Designing a Database Schema You Won't Regret in Six Months
item.description: Schema design choices are quiet for the first month and loud for the next five years. A practical guide to the decisions that matter most early.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: GraphQL vs REST: A Practical Comparison
item.description: Beyond the hype — when GraphQL is genuinely the right choice, when REST stays the obvious answer, and what changes when you actually run them in production.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: How to Write React Components That Don't Get Refactored Twice
item.description: Concrete patterns for writing React components that survive contact with a real codebase — composition, prop design, and the small habits that prevent rewrites.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Debugging Modern Web Apps: A Field Guide
item.description: A real toolkit for finding bugs in 2026 — DevTools tactics, network inspection, source maps, structured logs, and the discipline that turns hours of guessing into ten minutes of looking.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: How to Use Git Confidently in a Team
item.description: Beyond add, commit, push — the Git workflows, recovery techniques, and team conventions that prevent the late-night merge disasters.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Monolith vs Microservices: When to Choose What
item.description: An honest look at the architectural debate that won't go away — when a monolith is the right call, when microservices earn their complexity, and the modular middle ground most teams should start with.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Understanding JavaScript Promises and Async/Await
item.description: A clear, example-led guide to async JavaScript — what Promises are, why async/await exists, the patterns that hold up, and the mistakes that quietly cost performance.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Effective Code Reviews That Don't Drain Your Team
item.description: How to give and receive code reviews that improve quality, raise people up, and don't turn into bottlenecks. Specific habits, anti-patterns, and the language that defuses friction.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Using AI Coding Assistants Without Losing Your Edge
item.description: AI is making engineers faster — and, for some, lazier. A practical guide to using AI assistants in your workflow while staying sharp, employable, and capable of solving the hard stuff.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: An Honest Guide to Picking a Frontend Framework in 2026
item.description: React, Next.js, Svelte, Solid, Astro, and the rest — a practical comparison of where each framework fits, written for people choosing one for a real project.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: Building Accessible Web Apps: A Practical Guide
item.description: Accessibility isn't checklist work — it's product quality. A working developer's guide to the WCAG basics, the tools that catch most issues, and the patterns that hold up.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial

item.title: How to Structure a Large React Application Without It Becoming a Maze
item.description: Folder structures, module boundaries, and team conventions that keep React projects navigable as they grow from one engineer to twenty.
item.author: BuildStack Editorial
